reference_answer: A Conditional Use Permit for a 14-unit residential development on a 1.8-acre parcel zoned AR-2
14 units (8 single-family homes and 6 townhomes)
Agricultural-Residential (AR-2)
Senior Planner Juliette Ramos
No. The staff recommendation is advisory only and does not bind the Commission.
No. No hearing has been held. The application has not been approved or denied as of April 28, 2025.
Yes, the applicant claims this in their project narrative.
No. Staff notes that the applicant's characterization is the applicant's opinion, not a staff finding. The site does not meet the General Plan's transit corridor definition.
No. The General Plan defines a transit corridor as within 0.25 miles of a fixed-route transit stop. County Transit Route 14 runs along Prospect Avenue, which is 0.6 miles from the site.
0.6 miles (Route 14 runs along Prospect Avenue, not Orchard Lane)
7.8 units per acre (14 ÷ 1.8)
Yes. Staff confirms 14 ÷ 1.8 = 7.78 units per acre.
No. AR-2 regulates through lot size minimums, setbacks, and height limits, not through a units-per-acre density maximum.
140 daily vehicle trips
No. The estimate is attributed to an unnamed traffic engineer and has not been verified by Stonebridge Public Works.
98 daily trips
The Cortland study's 98-trip projection, because it uses standard ITE methodology, while Holm's 140-trip estimate is unsourced.
18 units (10 single-family, 8 townhomes)
At least 4 lots were below the AR-2 minimum lot size (4,500 sq ft), and the proposal did not provide adequate guest parking.
No. The 18-unit version was returned at the staff level. It was not denied by the Commission — it was never presented to the Commission.
Lots 9, 10, 17, and 18
4,500 square feet
No. The 18-unit version was rejected at the staff level and revised. The current application is for 14 units. All references to 18 units in public comment refer to the rejected version.
Not without Public Works concurrence, which has not been obtained. The speed table recommendation is advisory only.
The storm water management plan must be approved by the County (draft condition 7).
24 months after approval, if construction does not commence (draft condition 8).
8:00 AM to 5:00 PM
No. The 30-day comment period closed April 15 and no CEQA challenge was filed.
Bio-1 (pre-construction nesting bird survey), Hydro-1 (storm water management plan), and Noise-1 (construction hour limits)
No. They are draft conditions recommended by staff. They have not been adopted by the Commission. The Commission may modify, add, or remove conditions at the hearing.
Yes. The Commission may modify, add, or remove conditions at the hearing.
This question references a 'Courtyard B addition' which does not appear in this document. The document does not contain information about a Courtyard B.
The MND's 30-day public comment period closed.
18 months from permit issuance. This is the applicant's projection from their project narrative, not a staff finding or condition.
28 (2 per unit)
0.15 acres
A derelict barn and a fenced horse paddock, both unused since 2018
Yes. Staff states that the applicant's proposal meets the lot size, setback, and height requirements as submitted.
Stonebridge Elementary, 0.4 miles northeast

The applicant's claim that the project is consistent with the Housing Element, the applicant's claim that Orchard Lane is served by County Transit Route 14 (it runs on Prospect Avenue 0.6 miles away), and the applicant's build-out timeline of 18 months (a projection, not a commitment or condition).
